questions.questionText: Social smile in infants first appears at:
questions.explanation: Social smile: 6-8 weeks (2 months) — first true social response. Developmental milestones: 3 months: head control, follows 180°, cooing. 6 months: sits with support, reaches/grasps, babbling. 9 months: sits alone, crawls, pincer grasp, stranger anxiety. 12 months: stands alone, walks with support, 1-2 words, waves bye. 18 months: walks alone, builds 3-block tower, 10 words. 24 months: runs, 6-block tower, 2-word sentences, knows body parts. Reference: OP Ghai — Developmental Milestones.

questions.optionB: 5 months (birth weight triples by 1 year, quadruples by 2 years)
questions.explanation: Weight milestones: Birth weight (~3 kg): physiological weight loss in first 3-5 days (up to 10%). Regained by 10-14 days. Doubles: 5 months. Triples: 1 year (~9 kg). Quadruples: 2 years (~12 kg). Daily weight gain: 30g/day (0-3 months), 20g/day (3-6 months), 10g/day (6-12 months). Height: birth ~50 cm, 1 year ~75 cm (1.5x), 4 years ~100 cm (2x birth). Head circumference: birth ~35 cm, 1 year ~47 cm. Anterior fontanelle closes: 12-18 months. Posterior: 2-4 months. Reference: OP Ghai — Growth.

questions.questionText: The best indicator of acute malnutrition in children is:
questions.optionB: Weight for height (wasting — indicates acute/recent malnutrition, MUAC <11.5 cm also indicates severe acute malnutrition)
questions.explanation: Nutritional assessment anthropometry: Weight for height (W/H): wasting — indicator of ACUTE malnutrition (<-2 SD: moderate wasting, <-3 SD: severe wasting/SAM). Height for age (H/A): stunting — indicator of CHRONIC malnutrition (<-2 SD: stunted). Weight for age (W/A): underweight — composite (both acute and chronic). MUAC: mid-upper arm circumference (<11.5 cm in 6-59 months: SAM, 11.5-12.5 cm: MAM). BMI for age: overweight/obesity assessment. WHO growth standards: used for children <5 years. Reference: OP Ghai — Nutritional Assessment.
